desc: Kampot is a laid-back river town in southern Cambodia that works as a base for pepper-farm country, Bokor highland viewpoints, and nearby Kep's seafood coast. It is best when you want low-intensity days with one or two active excursions, especially in the drier months from roughly December to April.
hls: Colonial-era riverside core with walkable café and market districts
Bokor National Park viewpoints, cloud-forest roads, and old hill-station ruins
Farm and food experiences built around Kampot pepper production
Easy pairing with Kep crab market and Rabbit Island beach day trips
Sunset river cruises, paddle routes, and countryside scooter loops
Strong value outside peak winter weeks

todo.name: Bokor National Park ridge day
todo.desc: Start early for cool conditions and combine Popokvil waterfall area, Lok Yeay Mao monument, and cliff viewpoints around Bokor Hill Station; if visibility is clear, add short ridge walks near roadside lookouts and plan a weather fallback because fog can close panoramas quickly.

todo.name: Phnom Chhngok cave temple stop
todo.desc: Visit the cave shrine with a local guide and allow time for steep limestone steps; pair it with nearby rice-field roads for a half-day countryside loop and avoid peak midday heat on the exposed approach.

todo.name: Pepper-farm deep dive
todo.desc: Book a guided session at La Plantation or a similar certified farm to compare red/black/white pepper processing, then build a tasting-focused lunch stop around Kampot pepper crab or pepper beef dishes in town.

todo.name: Kep National Park + coast combo
todo.desc: Do the easy-moderate perimeter trail in Kep National Park (roughly 7–8 km with rolling elevation), then continue to Kep crab market or Rabbit Island boats for a mixed active/coastal day.

todo.name: Prek Tuek Chhu river paddle
todo.desc: Choose sunrise or late-afternoon kayaking/SUP on quieter river sections to reduce heat exposure, and add sunset at Kampot old bridge or riverside promenades back in town.

todo.name: Flexible weather buffer day
todo.desc: Keep one uncommitted day for monsoon showers or cloud cover shifts—ideal for café work blocks, cooking class alternatives, or rescheduling Bokor viewpoints.

wifi.note: Most boutique stays and central cafés are reliable for routine remote work; have a backup SIM for countryside trips where coverage can dip.

prac.fltNote: No nonstop UK-Kampot route. Typical itineraries fly London to Phnom Penh (often one-stop) then transfer 3-4 hours by road; return fares are commonly ~£500-£900 off-peak and can climb above ~£950 in Christmas/New Year and July-August demand windows.
prac.currency: Cambodian riel (KHR) and US dollars circulate in parallel; confirm card acceptance and keep small change for local transport/markets.
prac.lang: Khmer is dominant; tourist-facing English is common in central Kampot and major farm operators.
prac.visa: Most visitors can use Cambodia eVisa or visa on arrival; passport validity and onward/onward-proof rules should be checked before departure.
bestFor: Slow travel and scooter-based countryside exploration
alerts: Road surfaces to Bokor and rural farm lanes can become slick in wet-season downpours.
Boat/kayak operations may pause during thunderstorms or high river flow.
Bring cash backups because smaller operators sometimes add card surcharges or do not accept cards.

sweet: December to March is usually the easiest window for clear Bokor viewpoints, manageable humidity, and reliable activity operations; April-May is hotter, while June-October is greener but rainier.
